feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: Third-year Mathematics student at the University of Washington, minoring in Applied Mathematics

entry.title: splitting algorithms
entry.content:
We develop algorithms aiming to solve the problem

\[\mathrm{min}_{x \in \mathcal{H}} f(x) + g(x)\]

we do not place restrictions on the smoothness of \(f\) or \(g\) aside from convexity and continuity.

Definition 1: A function is said to be closed if its epigraph (area over the curve) is closed. Definition 2: A function is proper convex if it is convex, doesn’t attain the value \(-\infty\), and is not identically \(\infty\).
entry.summary: I discuss basics on proximal splitting schemes for convex optimization. The main motivation is to provide background in the methods used by (Papadakis et al, 2013) to solve Benamou-Brenier's formulation of the Wasserstein distance.
